known_divergence: Async subagent lifecycle is aligned end-to-end: the parent gets the launched envelope, the subagent runs in the background (its file effect lands), and on completion the main loop re-enters with a <task-notification> and the parent's final result matches ('The background agent finished.'). Only num_turns differs (hare 2 vs reference 1): the release does not count the async launch tool_result as a turn, while hare counts every tool_result as a user turn. Fixing that cleanly needs a turn-counting change that touches all cases, so it is deferred rather than patched with a launched-message content heuristic.
